{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ninomiya Akagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja shaden.JPG |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Ninomiya Town|ja|二之宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Town|1=Ninomiyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682347}} 886 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.367244|N|139.167656|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage = |festival = {{ill|April 15|en|April 15|WD=Q2497|lt=April 15|1=April 15}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Ninomiya
Akagi Shrine|caption=|position=top|width=210|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|36|22|2|N|139|10|3.5|E}}}} |prefecture= {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|1=Maebashi}} |deity= {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} }} [[File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|Torii Gate]] '''Ninomiya Akagi Shrine''' (二宮赤城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Ninomiya Town|ja|二之宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Town|1=Ninomiyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682347}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and considered a disputed {{ill|Second Shrine of Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国二宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shrine of Kōzuke Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is one of approximately 300 Akagi Shrines across Japan, predominantly centered in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}, and is considered one of the main candidates for the original Akagi Shrine. == Deities == * {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}) * Several other deities. == History == [[File:MountAkagi.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} (worshipped as a sacred mountain)]] == Overview == The shrine's founding is unknown but is thought to date back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Historical records indicate that the {{ill|Kōzuke clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, resided at the southern base of Mount Akagi and are believed to have been involved in the shrine's establishment. The title "Second Shrine" (二宮) is believed to have been adopted around the 12th century"Ninomiya Akagi Shrine," in ''Gunma Prefecture Place names''.. The {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} record several instances of Rank conferrals to "Akagi Shrine." Additionally, the shrine appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} named "Akagi Shrine of Setagōri, Kōzuke Province." The shrine is one of the disputed sites for this listing, along with {{ill|Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the mountainside and {{ill|Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja|大洞赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the summit. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the shrine fell into decline under {{ill|Hōjō Ujinao|en|Hōjō Ujinao|WD=Q1156668|lt=Hōjō Ujinao|1=Hōjō Ujinao}} but was restored under the {{ill|Makino clan|en|Makino clan|WD=Q2975316|lt=Makino clan|1=Makino clan}}, who governed {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Efforts were made to unify Ninomiya, Miyosawa, and Ōdo Akagi Shrines into a single {{ill|Kokuhē Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhē Chūsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, but these plans were not realized due to the end of World War II. === typeings === Several records from the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} document Rank conferrals to "Akagi Shrine" (with possible attributions to other sites). * 839 (6th year of Shōwa): Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (''Shoku Nihon Kōki'') * 867 (9th year of Jōgan): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 869 (11th year of Jōgan): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 874 (16th year of Jōgan): Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 880 (4th year of Gangyō): Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''), noted as "Akagi Ishigami" or "Akagi Numagami."Different manuscripts vary between "Akagi Ishigami" and "Akagi Numagami." === Role in Akagi Worship === {{seealso|Akagi Shrine#History}} The title "Akagi Shrine" is shared by numerous shrines centered in Gunma Prefecture. The {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' is disputed among Ninomiya Akagi Shrine, Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, and Ōdo Akagi Shrine. Ninomiya Akagi Shrine, located at the mountain's base, is unique for retaining the title "Second Shrine" (二宮). This distinction suggests its prominence during the Kamakura period as a principal center of Akagi worshipKeio Tajima, "Akagi Shrine," in ''The Deities of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 11, Kantō Region'' (Hakuraku Publishing).. Today, Akagi worship centers on Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, though the connection between the two shrines remains strong, as evidenced by biannual {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} processions in April and November. == Grounds == The grounds are surrounded by moats and earthen embankments, preserving a medieval shrine layout designated as a municipal historic siteInformation board on the grounds.. Only used during Goshinkō. Designated December 24, 1975. * Ema (Votive Pictures) - Four paintings donated by Maebashi Domain and Ōgo Domain lords. Approximately a 7-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Akagi Shrines|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja|大洞赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ninomiya-akagi-jinja|二宮赤城神社}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250201.html Akagi Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Archives") {{translated page|ja|二宮赤城神社|version=103617639|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Ninomiya-akagi-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ninomiya Akagi Shrine (Q17220826)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kōzuke Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|lt=Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|commons|Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja|WD=Q17220826|1=Ninomiya Akagi Shrine}} || — || — || {{ill|Ni-no-Miya|WD=Q134917533|lt=Ni-no-Miya|1=Ni-no-Miya}} || {{ill|Akagi Shrine|lt=Akagi Shrine|WD=Q135039965|1=Akagi Shrine}} || {{coord|36.367244|139.167656|display=inline}} || — |} {{wikidata link|Q17220826}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja]] [[da:d:Q17220826]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja]] [[Category:Akagi Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Maebashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:pages]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ninomiya Akagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja shaden.JPG |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Ninomiya Town|ja|二之宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Town|1=Ninomiyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682347}} 886 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.367244|N|139.167656|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage = |festival = {{ill|April 15|en|April 15|WD=Q2497|lt=April 15|1=April 15}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Ninomiya
Akagi Shrine|caption=|position=top|width=210|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|36|22|2|N|139|10|3.5|E}}}} |prefecture= {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|1=Maebashi}} |deity= {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} }} [[File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|Torii Gate]] '''Ninomiya Akagi Shrine''' (二宮赤城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Ninomiya Town|ja|二之宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Town|1=Ninomiyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682347}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and considered a disputed {{ill|Second Shrine of Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国二宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shrine of Kōzuke Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is one of approximately 300 Akagi Shrines across Japan, predominantly centered in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}, and is considered one of the main candidates for the original Akagi Shrine. == Deities == * {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}) * Several other deities. == History == [[File:MountAkagi.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} (worshipped as a sacred mountain)]] == Overview == The shrine's founding is unknown but is thought to date back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Historical records indicate that the {{ill|Kōzuke clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, resided at the southern base of Mount Akagi and are believed to have been involved in the shrine's establishment. The title "Second Shrine" (二宮) is believed to have been adopted around the 12th century"Ninomiya Akagi Shrine," in ''Gunma Prefecture Place names''.. The {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} record several instances of Rank conferrals to "Akagi Shrine." Additionally, the shrine appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} named "Akagi Shrine of Setagōri, Kōzuke Province." The shrine is one of the disputed sites for this listing, along with {{ill|Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the mountainside and {{ill|Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja|大洞赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the summit. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the shrine fell into decline under {{ill|Hōjō Ujinao|en|Hōjō Ujinao|WD=Q1156668|lt=Hōjō Ujinao|1=Hōjō Ujinao}} but was restored under the {{ill|Makino clan|en|Makino clan|WD=Q2975316|lt=Makino clan|1=Makino clan}}, who governed {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Efforts were made to unify Ninomiya, Miyosawa, and Ōdo Akagi Shrines into a single {{ill|Kokuhē Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhē Chūsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, but these plans were not realized due to the end of World War II. === typeings === Several records from the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} document Rank conferrals to "Akagi Shrine" (with possible attributions to other sites). * 839 (6th year of Shōwa): Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (''Shoku Nihon Kōki'') * 867 (9th year of Jōgan): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 869 (11th year of Jōgan): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 874 (16th year of Jōgan): Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 880 (4th year of Gangyō): Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''), noted as "Akagi Ishigami" or "Akagi Numagami."Different manuscripts vary between "Akagi Ishigami" and "Akagi Numagami." === Role in Akagi Worship === {{seealso|Akagi Shrine#History}} The title "Akagi Shrine" is shared by numerous shrines centered in Gunma Prefecture. The {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' is disputed among Ninomiya Akagi Shrine, Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, and Ōdo Akagi Shrine. Ninomiya Akagi Shrine, located at the mountain's base, is unique for retaining the title "Second Shrine" (二宮). This distinction suggests its prominence during the Kamakura period as a principal center of Akagi worshipKeio Tajima, "Akagi Shrine," in ''The Deities of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 11, Kantō Region'' (Hakuraku Publishing).. Today, Akagi worship centers on Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, though the connection between the two shrines remains strong, as evidenced by biannual {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} processions in April and November. == Grounds == The grounds are surrounded by moats and earthen embankments, preserving a medieval shrine layout designated as a municipal historic siteInformation board on the grounds.. File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja houtou.JPG|Treasury (municipal cultural property) File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja shourou.JPG|Bell Tower (municipal cultural property) == Festivals == * Goshinkō (April and December, on the first "Tatsu" day)Festival details are from the information board on-site. - The sacred object is transported to Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, recognized as an important intangible cultural property. * Goshinji (March and November, on the final "Uma" day) * festival (April 15) == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Nasori Mask (納曽利面) - A theatrical mask believed to date back to the 1453 shrine restoration. Designated May 6, 1969[http://www.manabi.pref.gunma.jp/bunkazai/c601099.htm Nasori Mask] (Gunma Prefectural Lifelong Learning Center).. === Maebashi City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Bonshō (Temple Bell) - Cast in 1623 for the Akagiyama Jingu-ji temple. Only used during Goshinkō. Designated December 24, 1975. * Ema (Votive Pictures) - Four paintings donated by Maebashi Domain and Ōgo Domain lords. Designated December 24, 1975. * Treasury - A structure from the Nanboku-chō period. Designated April 25, 1983. '''Historic Sites''' * Ninomiya Akagi Shrine Grounds - Designated March 12, 1984. '''Intangible Cultural Properties''' * Goshinkō Festival - Designated April 16, 1993. * Ninomiya Akagi Shrine’s Daidai Kagura - Designated March 24, 2009. * Ninomiya’s Shiki Sanbansō - Designated February 10, 1970. == Information == '''location''' * {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}, Ninomiya Town 886 '''Access''' * From {{ill|Maebashi Station|en|Maebashi Station|WD=Q1062416|lt=Maebashi Station|1=Maebashi Station}} (JR East {{ill|Ryōmō Line|en|Ryōmō Line|WD=Q253391|lt=Ryōmō Line|1=Ryōmō Line}}), take a bus bound for "Higashi-Ōmuro" and alight at "Ninomiya Town, Shrine Back." Approximately a 7-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Akagi Shrines|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja|大洞赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ninomiya-akagi-jinja|二宮赤城神社}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250201.html Akagi Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Archives") {{translated page|ja|二宮赤城神社|version=103617639|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Ninomiya-akagi-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ninomiya Akagi Shrine (Q17220826)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kōzuke Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|lt=Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|commons|Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja|WD=Q17220826|1=Ninomiya Akagi Shrine}} || — || — || {{ill|Ni-no-Miya|WD=Q134917533|lt=Ni-no-Miya|1=Ni-no-Miya}} || {{ill|Akagi Shrine|lt=Akagi Shrine|WD=Q135039965|1=Akagi Shrine}} || {{coord|36.367244|139.167656|display=inline}} || — |} {{wikidata link|Q17220826}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja]] [[da:d:Q17220826]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja]] [[Category:Akagi Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Maebashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:pages]]

The shrine is one of approximately 300 Akagi Shrines across Japan, predominantly centered in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}, and is considered one of the main candidates for the original Akagi Shrine. == Deities == * {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}) * Several other deities. == History == [[File:MountAkagi.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} (worshipped as a sacred mountain)]] == Overview == The shrine's founding is unknown but is thought to date back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Historical records indicate that the {{ill|Kōzuke clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, resided at the southern base of Mount Akagi and are believed to have been involved in the shrine's establishment. The title "Second Shrine" (二宮) is believed to have been adopted around the 12th century"Ninomiya Akagi Shrine," in ''Gunma Prefecture Place names''.. The {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} record several instances of Rank conferrals to "Akagi Shrine." Additionally, the shrine appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} named "Akagi Shrine of Setagōri, Kōzuke Province." The shrine is one of the disputed sites for this listing, along with {{ill|Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the mountainside and {{ill|Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja|大洞赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the summit. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the shrine fell into decline under {{ill|Hōjō Ujinao|en|Hōjō Ujinao|WD=Q1156668|lt=Hōjō Ujinao|1=Hōjō Ujinao}} but was restored under the {{ill|Makino clan|en|Makino clan|WD=Q2975316|lt=Makino clan|1=Makino clan}}, who governed {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Efforts were made to unify Ninomiya, Miyosawa, and Ōdo Akagi Shrines into a single {{ill|Kokuhē Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhē Chūsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, but these plans were not realized due to the end of World War II. === typeings === Several records from the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} document Rank conferrals to "Akagi Shrine" (with possible attributions to other sites). * 839 (6th year of Shōwa): Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (''Shoku Nihon Kōki'') * 867 (9th year of Jōgan): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 869 (11th year of Jōgan): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 874 (16th year of Jōgan): Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 880 (4th year of Gangyō): Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''), noted as "Akagi Ishigami" or "Akagi Numagami."Different manuscripts vary between "Akagi Ishigami" and "Akagi Numagami." === Role in Akagi Worship === {{seealso|Akagi Shrine#History}} The title "Akagi Shrine" is shared by numerous shrines centered in Gunma Prefecture. The {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' is disputed among Ninomiya Akagi Shrine, Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, and Ōdo Akagi Shrine. Ninomiya Akagi Shrine, located at the mountain's base, is unique for retaining the title "Second Shrine" (二宮). This distinction suggests its prominence during the Kamakura period as a principal center of Akagi worshipKeio Tajima, "Akagi Shrine," in ''The Deities of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 11, Kantō Region'' (Hakuraku Publishing).. Today, Akagi worship centers on Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, though the connection between the two shrines remains strong, as evidenced by biannual {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} processions in April and November. == Grounds == The grounds are surrounded by moats and earthen embankments, preserving a medieval shrine layout designated as a municipal historic siteInformation board on the grounds.. File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja houtou.JPG|Treasury (municipal cultural property) File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja shourou.JPG|Bell Tower (municipal cultural property) == Festivals == * Goshinkō (April and December, on the first "Tatsu" day)Festival details are from the information board on-site. - The sacred object is transported to Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, recognized as an important intangible cultural property. * Goshinji (March and November, on the final "Uma" day) * festival (April 15) == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Nasori Mask (納曽利面) - A theatrical mask believed to date back to the 1453 shrine restoration. Designated May 6, 1969[http://www.manabi.pref.gunma.jp/bunkazai/c601099.htm Nasori Mask] (Gunma Prefectural Lifelong Learning Center).. === Maebashi City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Bonshō (Temple Bell) - Cast in 1623 for the Akagiyama Jingu-ji temple. Only used during Goshinkō. Designated December 24, 1975. * Ema (Votive Pictures) - Four paintings donated by Maebashi Domain and Ōgo Domain lords. Designated December 24, 1975. * Treasury - A structure from the Nanboku-chō period. Designated April 25, 1983. '''Historic Sites''' * Ninomiya Akagi Shrine Grounds - Designated March 12, 1984. '''Intangible Cultural Properties''' * Goshinkō Festival - Designated April 16, 1993. * Ninomiya Akagi Shrine’s Daidai Kagura - Designated March 24, 2009. * Ninomiya’s Shiki Sanbansō - Designated February 10, 1970. == Information == '''location''' * {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}, Ninomiya Town 886 '''Access''' * From {{ill|Maebashi Station|en|Maebashi Station|WD=Q1062416|lt=Maebashi Station|1=Maebashi Station}} (JR East {{ill|Ryōmō Line|en|Ryōmō Line|WD=Q253391|lt=Ryōmō Line|1=Ryōmō Line}}), take a bus bound for "Higashi-Ōmuro" and alight at "Ninomiya Town, Shrine Back." Approximately a 7-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Akagi Shrines|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja|大洞赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ninomiya-akagi-jinja|二宮赤城神社}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250201.html Akagi Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Archives") {{translated page|ja|二宮赤城神社|version=103617639|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Ninomiya-akagi-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ninomiya Akagi Shrine (Q17220826)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kōzuke Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|lt=Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|commons|Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja|WD=Q17220826|1=Ninomiya Akagi Shrine}} || — || — || {{ill|Ni-no-Miya|WD=Q134917533|lt=Ni-no-Miya|1=Ni-no-Miya}} || {{ill|Akagi Shrine|lt=Akagi Shrine|WD=Q135039965|1=Akagi Shrine}} || {{coord|36.367244|139.167656|display=inline}} || — |} {{wikidata link|Q17220826}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja]] [[da:d:Q17220826]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja]] [[Category:Akagi Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Maebashi]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:pages]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ninomiya Akagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja shaden.JPG |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Ninomiya Town|ja|二之宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Town|1=Ninomiyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682347}} 886 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.367244|N|139.167656|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage = |festival = {{ill|April 15|en|April 15|WD=Q2497|lt=April 15|1=April 15}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Ninomiya
Akagi Shrine|caption=|position=top|width=210|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|36|22|2|N|139|10|3.5|E}}}} |prefecture= {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|1=Maebashi}} |deity= {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} }} [[File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|Torii Gate]] '''Ninomiya Akagi Shrine''' (二宮赤城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Ninomiya Town|ja|二之宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Town|1=Ninomiyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682347}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and considered a disputed {{ill|Second Shrine of Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国二宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shrine of Kōzuke Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is one of approximately 300 Akagi Shrines across Japan, predominantly centered in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}, and is considered one of the main candidates for the original Akagi Shrine. == Deities == * {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}) * Several other deities. == History == [[File:MountAkagi.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} (worshipped as a sacred mountain)]] == Overview == The shrine's founding is unknown but is thought to date back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Historical records indicate that the {{ill|Kōzuke clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, resided at the southern base of Mount Akagi and are believed to have been involved in the shrine's establishment. The title "Second Shrine" (二宮) is believed to have been adopted around the 12th century"Ninomiya Akagi Shrine," in ''Gunma Prefecture Place names''.. The {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} record several instances of Rank conferrals to "Akagi Shrine." Additionally, the shrine appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} named "Akagi Shrine of Setagōri, Kōzuke Province." The shrine is one of the disputed sites for this listing, along with {{ill|Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the mountainside and {{ill|Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja|大洞赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the summit. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the shrine fell into decline under {{ill|Hōjō Ujinao|en|Hōjō Ujinao|WD=Q1156668|lt=Hōjō Ujinao|1=Hōjō Ujinao}} but was restored under the {{ill|Makino clan|en|Makino clan|WD=Q2975316|lt=Makino clan|1=Makino clan}}, who governed {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Efforts were made to unify Ninomiya, Miyosawa, and Ōdo Akagi Shrines into a single {{ill|Kokuhē Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhē Chūsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, but these plans were not realized due to the end of World War II. === typeings === Several records from the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} document Rank conferrals to "Akagi Shrine" (with possible attributions to other sites). * 839 (6th year of Shōwa): Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (''Shoku Nihon Kōki'') * 867 (9th year of Jōgan): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 869 (11th year of Jōgan): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 874 (16th year of Jōgan): Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 880 (4th year of Gangyō): Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''), noted as "Akagi Ishigami" or "Akagi Numagami."Different manuscripts vary between "Akagi Ishigami" and "Akagi Numagami." === Role in Akagi Worship === {{seealso|Akagi Shrine#History}} The title "Akagi Shrine" is shared by numerous shrines centered in Gunma Prefecture. The {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' is disputed among Ninomiya Akagi Shrine, Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, and Ōdo Akagi Shrine. Ninomiya Akagi Shrine, located at the mountain's base, is unique for retaining the title "Second Shrine" (二宮). This distinction suggests its prominence during the Kamakura period as a principal center of Akagi worshipKeio Tajima, "Akagi Shrine," in ''The Deities of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 11, Kantō Region'' (Hakuraku Publishing).. Today, Akagi worship centers on Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, though the connection between the two shrines remains strong, as evidenced by biannual {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} processions in April and November. == Grounds == The grounds are surrounded by moats and earthen embankments, preserving a medieval shrine layout designated as a municipal historic siteInformation board on the grounds.. File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja houtou.JPG|Treasury (municipal cultural property) File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja shourou.JPG|Bell Tower (municipal cultural property) == Festivals == * Goshinkō (April and December, on the first "Tatsu" day)Festival details are from the information board on-site. - The sacred object is transported to Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, recognized as an important intangible cultural property. * Goshinji (March and November, on the final "Uma" day) * festival (April 15) == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Nasori Mask (納曽利面) - A theatrical mask believed to date back to the 1453 shrine restoration. Designated May 6, 1969[http://www.manabi.pref.gunma.jp/bunkazai/c601099.htm Nasori Mask] (Gunma Prefectural Lifelong Learning Center).. === Maebashi City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Bonshō (Temple Bell) - Cast in 1623 for the Akagiyama Jingu-ji temple. Only used during Goshinkō. Designated December 24, 1975. * Ema (Votive Pictures) - Four paintings donated by Maebashi Domain and Ōgo Domain lords. Designated December 24, 1975. * Treasury - A structure from the Nanboku-chō period. Designated April 25, 1983. '''Historic Sites''' * Ninomiya Akagi Shrine Grounds - Designated March 12, 1984. '''Intangible Cultural Properties''' * Goshinkō Festival - Designated April 16, 1993. * Ninomiya Akagi Shrine’s Daidai Kagura - Designated March 24, 2009. * Ninomiya’s Shiki Sanbansō - Designated February 10, 1970. == Information == '''location''' * {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}, Ninomiya Town 886 '''Access''' * From {{ill|Maebashi Station|en|Maebashi Station|WD=Q1062416|lt=Maebashi Station|1=Maebashi Station}} (JR East {{ill|Ryōmō Line|en|Ryōmō Line|WD=Q253391|lt=Ryōmō Line|1=Ryōmō Line}}), take a bus bound for "Higashi-Ōmuro" and alight at "Ninomiya Town, Shrine Back." Approximately a 7-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Akagi Shrines|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja|大洞赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ninomiya-akagi-jinja|二宮赤城神社}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250201.html Akagi Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Archives") {{translated page|ja|二宮赤城神社|version=103617639|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Ninomiya-akagi-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ninomiya Akagi Shrine (Q17220826)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kōzuke Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|lt=Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|commons|Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja|WD=Q17220826|1=Ninomiya Akagi Shrine}} || — || — || {{ill|Ni-no-Miya|WD=Q134917533|lt=Ni-no-Miya|1=Ni-no-Miya}} || {{ill|Akagi Shrine|lt=Akagi Shrine|WD=Q135039965|1=Akagi Shrine}} || {{coord|36.367244|139.167656|display=inline}} || — |} {{wikidata link|Q17220826}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja]] [[da:d:Q17220826]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja]] [[Category:Akagi Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Maebashi]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ninomiya Akagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja shaden.JPG |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Ninomiya Town|ja|二之宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Town|1=Ninomiyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682347}} 886 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.367244|N|139.167656|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage = |festival = {{ill|April 15|en|April 15|WD=Q2497|lt=April 15|1=April 15}} |Rituals = |Map = {{location map|Japan Gunma|label=Ninomiya
Akagi Shrine|caption=|position=top|width=210|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|36|22|2|N|139|10|3.5|E}}}} |prefecture= {{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|1=Maebashi}} |deity= {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}} }} [[File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|Torii Gate]] '''Ninomiya Akagi Shrine''' (二宮赤城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Ninomiya Town|ja|二之宮町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Town|1=Ninomiyamachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28682347}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and considered a disputed {{ill|Second Shrine of Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国二宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shrine of Kōzuke Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is one of approximately 300 Akagi Shrines across Japan, predominantly centered in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}, and is considered one of the main candidates for the original Akagi Shrine. == Deities == * {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}) * Several other deities. == History == [[File:MountAkagi.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} (worshipped as a sacred mountain)]] == Overview == The shrine's founding is unknown but is thought to date back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Historical records indicate that the {{ill|Kōzuke clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, resided at the southern base of Mount Akagi and are believed to have been involved in the shrine's establishment. The title "Second Shrine" (二宮) is believed to have been adopted around the 12th century"Ninomiya Akagi Shrine," in ''Gunma Prefecture Place names''.. The {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} record several instances of Rank conferrals to "Akagi Shrine." Additionally, the shrine appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} named "Akagi Shrine of Setagōri, Kōzuke Province." The shrine is one of the disputed sites for this listing, along with {{ill|Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the mountainside and {{ill|Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja|大洞赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the summit. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the shrine fell into decline under {{ill|Hōjō Ujinao|en|Hōjō Ujinao|WD=Q1156668|lt=Hōjō Ujinao|1=Hōjō Ujinao}} but was restored under the {{ill|Makino clan|en|Makino clan|WD=Q2975316|lt=Makino clan|1=Makino clan}}, who governed {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Efforts were made to unify Ninomiya, Miyosawa, and Ōdo Akagi Shrines into a single {{ill|Kokuhē Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhē Chūsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, but these plans were not realized due to the end of World War II. === typeings === Several records from the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} document Rank conferrals to "Akagi Shrine" (with possible attributions to other sites). * 839 (6th year of Shōwa): Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (''Shoku Nihon Kōki'') * 867 (9th year of Jōgan): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 869 (11th year of Jōgan): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 874 (16th year of Jōgan): Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 880 (4th year of Gangyō): Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''), noted as "Akagi Ishigami" or "Akagi Numagami."Different manuscripts vary between "Akagi Ishigami" and "Akagi Numagami." === Role in Akagi Worship === {{seealso|Akagi Shrine#History}} The title "Akagi Shrine" is shared by numerous shrines centered in Gunma Prefecture. The {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' is disputed among Ninomiya Akagi Shrine, Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, and Ōdo Akagi Shrine. Ninomiya Akagi Shrine, located at the mountain's base, is unique for retaining the title "Second Shrine" (二宮). This distinction suggests its prominence during the Kamakura period as a principal center of Akagi worshipKeio Tajima, "Akagi Shrine," in ''The Deities of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 11, Kantō Region'' (Hakuraku Publishing).. Today, Akagi worship centers on Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, though the connection between the two shrines remains strong, as evidenced by biannual {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} processions in April and November. == Grounds == The grounds are surrounded by moats and earthen embankments, preserving a medieval shrine layout designated as a municipal historic siteInformation board on the grounds.. File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja houtou.JPG|Treasury (municipal cultural property) File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja shourou.JPG|Bell Tower (municipal cultural property) == Festivals == * Goshinkō (April and December, on the first "Tatsu" day)Festival details are from the information board on-site. - The sacred object is transported to Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, recognized as an important intangible cultural property. * Goshinji (March and November, on the final "Uma" day) * festival (April 15) == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Nasori Mask (納曽利面) - A theatrical mask believed to date back to the 1453 shrine restoration. Designated May 6, 1969[http://www.manabi.pref.gunma.jp/bunkazai/c601099.htm Nasori Mask] (Gunma Prefectural Lifelong Learning Center).. === Maebashi City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Bonshō (Temple Bell) - Cast in 1623 for the Akagiyama Jingu-ji temple. Only used during Goshinkō. Designated December 24, 1975. * Ema (Votive Pictures) - Four paintings donated by Maebashi Domain and Ōgo Domain lords. Designated December 24, 1975. * Treasury - A structure from the Nanboku-chō period. Designated April 25, 1983. '''Historic Sites''' * Ninomiya Akagi Shrine Grounds - Designated March 12, 1984. '''Intangible Cultural Properties''' * Goshinkō Festival - Designated April 16, 1993. * Ninomiya Akagi Shrine’s Daidai Kagura - Designated March 24, 2009. * Ninomiya’s Shiki Sanbansō - Designated February 10, 1970. == Information == '''location''' * {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}, Ninomiya Town 886 '''Access''' * From {{ill|Maebashi Station|en|Maebashi Station|WD=Q1062416|lt=Maebashi Station|1=Maebashi Station}} (JR East {{ill|Ryōmō Line|en|Ryōmō Line|WD=Q253391|lt=Ryōmō Line|1=Ryōmō Line}}), take a bus bound for "Higashi-Ōmuro" and alight at "Ninomiya Town, Shrine Back." Approximately a 7-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Akagi Shrines|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja|大洞赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ninomiya-akagi-jinja|二宮赤城神社}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250201.html Akagi Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Archives") {{translated page|ja|二宮赤城神社|version=103617639|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Ninomiya-akagi-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ninomiya Akagi Shrine (Q17220826)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kōzuke Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|lt=Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|commons|Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja|WD=Q17220826|1=Ninomiya Akagi Shrine}} || — || — || {{ill|Ni-no-Miya|WD=Q134917533|lt=Ni-no-Miya|1=Ni-no-Miya}} || {{ill|Akagi Shrine|lt=Akagi Shrine|WD=Q135039965|1=Akagi Shrine}} || {{coord|36.367244|139.167656|display=inline}} || — |} {{wikidata link|Q17220826}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja]] [[da:d:Q17220826]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja]] [[Category:Akagi Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Maebashi]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]]

It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and considered a disputed {{ill|Second Shrine of Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国二宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shrine of Kōzuke Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it held the status of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is one of approximately 300 Akagi Shrines across Japan, predominantly centered in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}, and is considered one of the main candidates for the original Akagi Shrine. == Deities == * {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja|大己貴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (another name for {{ill|Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主|1=Ōkuninushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q276944}}) * Several other deities. == History == [[File:MountAkagi.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} (worshipped as a sacred mountain)]] == Overview == The shrine's founding is unknown but is thought to date back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Historical records indicate that the {{ill|Kōzuke clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, descendants of {{ill|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, resided at the southern base of Mount Akagi and are believed to have been involved in the shrine's establishment. The title "Second Shrine" (二宮) is believed to have been adopted around the 12th century"Ninomiya Akagi Shrine," in ''Gunma Prefecture Place names''.. The {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} record several instances of Rank conferrals to "Akagi Shrine." Additionally, the shrine appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} named "Akagi Shrine of Setagōri, Kōzuke Province." The shrine is one of the disputed sites for this listing, along with {{ill|Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the mountainside and {{ill|Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja|大洞赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at the summit. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the shrine fell into decline under {{ill|Hōjō Ujinao|en|Hōjō Ujinao|WD=Q1156668|lt=Hōjō Ujinao|1=Hōjō Ujinao}} but was restored under the {{ill|Makino clan|en|Makino clan|WD=Q2975316|lt=Makino clan|1=Makino clan}}, who governed {{ill|Ōgo Castle|ja|大胡城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgo Castle|1=Ogo Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438544}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Efforts were made to unify Ninomiya, Miyosawa, and Ōdo Akagi Shrines into a single {{ill|Kokuhē Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhē Chūsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, but these plans were not realized due to the end of World War II. === typeings === Several records from the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} document Rank conferrals to "Akagi Shrine" (with possible attributions to other sites). * 839 (6th year of Shōwa): Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (''Shoku Nihon Kōki'') * 867 (9th year of Jōgan): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 869 (11th year of Jōgan): Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 874 (16th year of Jōgan): Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * 880 (4th year of Gangyō): Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade (''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''), noted as "Akagi Ishigami" or "Akagi Numagami."Different manuscripts vary between "Akagi Ishigami" and "Akagi Numagami." === Role in Akagi Worship === {{seealso|Akagi Shrine#History}} The title "Akagi Shrine" is shared by numerous shrines centered in Gunma Prefecture. The {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' is disputed among Ninomiya Akagi Shrine, Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, and Ōdo Akagi Shrine. Ninomiya Akagi Shrine, located at the mountain's base, is unique for retaining the title "Second Shrine" (二宮). This distinction suggests its prominence during the Kamakura period as a principal center of Akagi worshipKeio Tajima, "Akagi Shrine," in ''The Deities of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 11, Kantō Region'' (Hakuraku Publishing).. Today, Akagi worship centers on Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, though the connection between the two shrines remains strong, as evidenced by biannual {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} processions in April and November. == Grounds == The grounds are surrounded by moats and earthen embankments, preserving a medieval shrine layout designated as a municipal historic siteInformation board on the grounds.. File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja haiden.JPG|Haiden File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja kaguraden.JPG|Kaguraden File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja houtou.JPG|Treasury (municipal cultural property) File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja zuishinmon.JPG|Zuishin Gate File:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja shourou.JPG|Bell Tower (municipal cultural property) == Festivals == * Goshinkō (April and December, on the first "Tatsu" day)Festival details are from the information board on-site. - The sacred object is transported to Miyosawa Akagi Shrine, recognized as an important intangible cultural property. * Goshinji (March and November, on the final "Uma" day) * festival (April 15) == Cultural Properties == === Gunma Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Nasori Mask (納曽利面) - A theatrical mask believed to date back to the 1453 shrine restoration. Designated May 6, 1969[http://www.manabi.pref.gunma.jp/bunkazai/c601099.htm Nasori Mask] (Gunma Prefectural Lifelong Learning Center).. === Maebashi City Designated Cultural Properties === '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Bonshō (Temple Bell) - Cast in 1623 for the Akagiyama Jingu-ji temple. Only used during Goshinkō. Designated December 24, 1975. * Ema (Votive Pictures) - Four paintings donated by Maebashi Domain and Ōgo Domain lords. Designated December 24, 1975. * Treasury - A structure from the Nanboku-chō period. Designated April 25, 1983. '''Historic Sites''' * Ninomiya Akagi Shrine Grounds - Designated March 12, 1984. '''Intangible Cultural Properties''' * Goshinkō Festival - Designated April 16, 1993. * Ninomiya Akagi Shrine’s Daidai Kagura - Designated March 24, 2009. * Ninomiya’s Shiki Sanbansō - Designated February 10, 1970. == Information == '''location''' * {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi City|1=Maebashi}}, Ninomiya Town 886 '''Access''' * From {{ill|Maebashi Station|en|Maebashi Station|WD=Q1062416|lt=Maebashi Station|1=Maebashi Station}} (JR East {{ill|Ryōmō Line|en|Ryōmō Line|WD=Q253391|lt=Ryōmō Line|1=Ryōmō Line}}), take a bus bound for "Higashi-Ōmuro" and alight at "Ninomiya Town, Shrine Back." Approximately a 7-minute walk. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Akagi Shrines|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} ** {{ill|Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja|三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** {{ill|Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja|大洞赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdo Akagi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Ninomiya-akagi-jinja|二宮赤城神社}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/250201.html Akagi Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Archives") {{translated page|ja|二宮赤城神社|version=103617639|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Ninomiya-akagi-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ninomiya Akagi Shrine (Q17220826)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kōzuke Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|lt=Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|commons|Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja|WD=Q17220826|1=Ninomiya Akagi Shrine}} || — || — || {{ill|Ni-no-Miya|WD=Q134917533|lt=Ni-no-Miya|1=Ni-no-Miya}} || {{ill|Akagi Shrine|lt=Akagi Shrine|WD=Q135039965|1=Akagi Shrine}} || {{coord|36.367244|139.167656|display=inline}} || — |} {{wikidata link|Q17220826}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja]] [[da:d:Q17220826]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Ninomiya-akagi-jinja]] [[Category:Akagi Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Maebashi]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]]
